frequency_714.title: SIGNAL TUNING
frequency_714.description: Tune the emergency frequency until the hidden transmission locks.

events.text: [OPENING CHANNEL 7.14]
No.
No, wait.
Someone actually answered.
Please don't be a recording.
If you are a recording, lie to me.
Actually don't. I am very tired of being lied to by electrical equipment.
Just send something back. Anything.
I will, but your signal is tearing apart.
If you want my name, keep the channel alive long enough for me to say it.
And if this is some kind of automated rescue protocol, congratulations. You have terrible timing.
Which is still better than no timing.
You sound like mission control.
Annoying. Useful, maybe. But annoying.
Fine. Verify me. I would verify me too.
I have been alone long enough to stop trusting my own echo.

events.title: EMERGENCY AUDIO TRANSCRIPT
events.content:
CHANNEL: 7.14 MHz
SIGNAL STATE: DISTORTED
VOICEPRINT: UNKNOWN FEMALE
RECOVERED FRAGMENTS:

"...anyone..."
"...relay is drifting..."
"...not Hale..."

Recommended action: manual signal tuning required.
events.caption: Text reconstruction of distorted emergency audio from channel 7.14.
events.message: [EMERGENCY AUDIO TRANSCRIPT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Signal is slipping.
events.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: The carrier keeps drifting like the relay has a death wish.
events.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Which, honestly, would fit the general personality of this station.
events.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: If you can tune it, do it now.
events.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Do not let that channel lead.
Tune it if you have to, but keep one hand on the exit.
And before you ask, yes, I hear how useless that advice is from inside a terminal.
Still. One hand on the exit.
[MAYA SIGNAL DEGRADING]
[SIGNAL LOCK FAILED]
No— don't let it drift.
events.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: I had you for a second. Don't make me go back to static.
events.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: [MANUAL RETUNE REQUIRED]
[SIGNAL LOCKED]
Finally...
Sorry.
Give me a second.
I forgot how to talk to people without wondering if they were already dead.
My name is Maya.
Maya Chen.
I was starting to think I'd die talking to static.
I spent three days talking to a maintenance drone once.
It never answered.
Still a better listener than Patel.
That was a joke. I think.
I am out of practice.
And if Ethan Hale is on your other channel, you need to stop listening to him.
You are speaking with Ethan Hale, aren't you?
Of course you are.
He always finds someone.
That sounds cruel. I know.
The problem with Ethan was never that he sounded dangerous.
He sounded reasonable.
He sounded like the only adult in the room.
And on ECHO, that was usually true.
That is what made it worse.
Listen carefully.
Hale is dead.
I am not saying that because I heard a rumor.
I am saying it because I saw what was left of the corridor.
Good.
No. Sorry. Not good.
I don't want you to believe me just because I sound scared.
Scared people can still be wrong.
I know how insane I sound. I know he's probably been calm, helpful, maybe even funny.
That's how it starts.
He explains the danger until you forget he might be part of it.
[MAYA TRUST INCREASED]
Of course.
He got to you first. That's the whole point.
I would've defended him too.
I did defend him.
More than once. Loudly. Stupidly.
I am not asking you to like me. I am asking you to survive long enough to doubt him.
Doubt is annoying. Doubt keeps you alive.
[MAYA TRUST DEGRADED]
[DUAL TRANSMISSION DETECTED]
Don't trust her.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Of course that's the first thing he says.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Maya—
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: No. Be quiet for once.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Operator, she once bypassed an alarm because it annoyed her.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: It was wrong.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: It was detecting decompression.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: And it was still wrong.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: See? This is what he does.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: He makes you argue about the small thing so you stop looking at the blood on the floor.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: That is wildly unfair.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Is it inaccurate?
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: That's what I thought.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Tell him he's an idiot.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I can hear her.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Good. Tell him twice.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Operator, this is not a conversation. This is a structural failure with opinions.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You don't know what you're doing.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I know exactly what happens when everyone lets you speak first.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: [HALE CHANNEL TEMPORARILY MUTED]
Thank you.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I know that probably felt wrong.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: He makes silence feel like betrayal.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I don't have much time, so I'm going to be blunt.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: [DUAL CHANNEL LOAD INCREASED]
Fine. Let him listen.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: But if he starts steering this conversation, I cut the channel myself.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: And Ethan, before you correct my terminology, yes, I know it is technically a carrier interruption.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I am calling it cutting the channel because it sounds better.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I was not going to correct that.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You absolutely were.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.title: SURVIVOR BIOMETRIC SCAN
events.content:
CHANNEL SOURCE: 7.14 MHz
VOICEPRINT MATCH: MAYA CHEN
LIFE SIGNS: ACTIVE
OXYGEN: 18%
LOCATION ESTIMATE: DAMAGED RELAY CONSOLE / LOWER ACCESS SPINE
VISUAL FEED: UNAVAILABLE
STATUS: ALIVE / CRITICAL
events.caption: Automated survivor verification generated from channel telemetry. No image recovered.
events.message: [SURVIVOR BIOMETRIC SCAN ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: That's me.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Alive.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Technically.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I've had better weeks.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Oxygen is at eighteen percent, which sounds better than it feels.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I started naming equipment yesterday. That's usually a bad sign.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: The cracked relay console is Malcolm.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Malcolm and I are not friends.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: But Malcolm is keeping me alive, so we're being professional.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Which is more than I can say for Hale.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: You want proof. Good. Keep wanting proof.
But understand this: the station is very good at giving you records after everyone who could explain them is gone.
You will find logs. You will find timestamps. You will find clean little labels for ugly things.
What you will not find is the sound Hale made when the corridor decompressed.
I still hear it anyway.
There were six of us.
Hale, Rourke, Lin, Patel, Arman... and me.
Rourke snored through pressure drills.
Lin labeled everything, including things that were already labels.
Patel pretended he hated everyone and fixed their coffee ratios when they weren't looking.
Arman sang when he was nervous.
He sang a lot near the end.
See? That's the part records don't keep.
They keep death times. Not people.

events.title: SURVIVOR STATEMENT: MAYA
events.content:
TEXT TRANSCRIPT / CHANNEL 7.14

MAYA CHEN:
"There were six assigned crew members on ECHO. After the breach, only one confirmed biological survivor remained: me. The signal using Ethan Hale's identity is not a living crew member. Do not allow it to control station routing."

Transcript confidence: 91%
events.caption: Text transcript reconstructed from Maya's stabilized emergency channel.

events.text: Me.
One confirmed biological survivor.
That is such a clean phrase, isn't it?
Biological survivor.
Like the station is politely avoiding the word alone.
And whatever is pretending to be Hale.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: NOT THE VICTIM
No.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: No, don't listen to that.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: [SIGNAL TRACE REVIEW]
TRACE: 7.14 MHz / DRIFTING CARRIER / FEMALE VOICEPRINT PARTIAL
events.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: You were close.
events.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Hold it steady when it bites. The drift gets worse right before the lock.
I hate that sentence. It sounds like something Hale would say.
[FREQUENCY TARGET CONFIRMED: 7.14]
How did I know Hale?
Unfortunately, thoroughly.
He corrected everybody. Reports. Logs. Messages. Grammar.
We almost spaced him for punctuation violations.
The annoying part is that he was right most of the time.
He could walk into a room, look at a failing system, and make you feel stupid for not hearing the problem breathing.
I hated that.
I miss that.
So when I tell you the thing on your channel is not Ethan Hale...
I am not saying it because I wanted him gone.
